places.name: Parque Quinta Normal Greenhouse
places.description: A beautiful deserted greenhouse in the middle of a park.

places.name: Bahá’í Temple of South America
places.description: A fantastical and futuristic temple of light built from nine monumental glass veils.

places.name: Museum of Memory and Human Rights
places.description: A museum founded to reflect on and remember the human rights abuses under Chilean dictator Augusto Pinochet.

places.name: Museo a Cielo Abierto
places.description: The gorgeous murals at this open-air museum form the largest street art collective in Chile.

places.name: National Astronomical Observatory of Chile
places.description: This observatory high on a hilltop in the center of Santiago got its start in 1852.

places.name: La Chascona
places.description: Pablo Neruda's custom-built lover's nest was made to remind him of the sea and named after his mistress' hair.

places.name: Museo Chileno de Arte Precolombino (Chilean Museum of Pre-Columbian Art)
places.description: This museum covers 10,000 years of Pre-Columbian Latin American history.

places.name: La Picá de Clinton
places.description: How a lunch counter in Chile became a shrine to the 42nd president of the United States

places.name: Willimapu Gastronomía Ancestral
places.description: In a corner of Santiago's largest market, this inventive Mapuche eatery shows "ancestral cuisine" doesn't have to mean old-fashioned.

places.name: 99 Restaurante
places.description: A rotating nine-course menu offers delicious deep-dives into the foodways of Chilean agricultural valleys.
